questions.prompt: A contractor holds a Residential General Contractor (RGC) endorsement. Under ORS 701.081, what are the minimum surety bond and general liability insurance amounts required?
questions.explanation: ORS 701.081 sets the RGC minimums at $25,000 surety bond and $500,000 general liability insurance. The $15,000/$100,000 figures apply to the Residential Limited Contractor (RLC) class, $20,000/$300,000 to Residential Specialty Contractor (RSC), and $80,000/$2,000,000 to the commercial CGC-1 endorsement under ORS 701.084.

questions.prompt: Under ORS 701.068, what authority does the CCB have when the total claims paid against a contractor's surety bond exceed the bond's penal sum?
choices: The CCB must immediately revoke the license permanently with no reinstatement option.
The CCB may suspend the license and may require a replacement bond of up to five times the ordinary amount before reinstatement.
The CCB may issue a written warning only; no suspension is permitted for a first occurrence.
The contractor's insurance policy automatically substitutes as the required bond until a replacement is filed.
questions.explanation: ORS 701.068 authorizes CCB to suspend the license when bond-paid obligations exceed the penal sum. Reinstatement may be conditioned on filing a new bond up to five times the ordinary endorsement amount. Permanent revocation on first occurrence is not the statutory default, and insurance does not substitute for the bond requirement.

questions.prompt: OAR 812-003-0200 requires a contractor's insurance certificate submitted to CCB to include which of the following?
choices: The contractor's federal employer identification number and payroll tax account number
The insurer's name, policy number, policy dates, required coverage amounts, products/completed-operations statement, and CCB as certificate holder
A signed acknowledgment from the property owner that the coverage is adequate for the project
The contractor's bonding company contact information and current bond balance
questions.explanation: OAR 812-003-0200 lists the required certificate fields: insurer name, policy number, policy effective and expiration dates, required coverage amounts, a statement of products/completed-operations coverage, and the CCB named as certificate holder. The other choices describe information not required by this rule.

questions.prompt: Under OAR 812-003-0142, a contractor submitting a new license application on August 1, 2025 should expect to pay which application fee?
choices: $200, the standard new-applicant fee that has been in effect since 2020
$325, the fee that applied to new applications before the 2025 transition
$480, a surcharge applies for applications filed in the second half of the calendar year
questions.explanation: OAR 812-003-0142 sets the new-license application fee at $400, effective 2025-07-01. A $325 prior fee applied to new applications before that date. Renewals and reissues moved to $400 earlier, effective 2024-07-01. There is no calendar-based surcharge in the rule.
